skill.name: sharepoint-audit
skill.description: Guide local SharePoint Online permission audits using PowerShell, Python, certificate authentication, and generated Markdown or HTML reports.
skill.summary: Run local SharePoint Online permission audits and produce sensitive access reports.
skill.author: Aqualia

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified local PowerShell and Python command execution guidance, which is central to this SharePoint audit workflow. Several high-severity matches are contextual mentions or Markdown formatting false positives, and no prompt injection or malicious exfiltration intent was found. Publish with a warning because the skill handles secrets, installs modules, and produces sensitive SharePoint audit reports.

medium_findings.title: Local External Command Execution Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run local PowerShell and Python audit commands. This is expected for the tool, but it can execute repository code on the operator machine and should only run from a trusted checkout.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain explicit local command invocations. The surrounding instructions show legitimate SharePoint audit intent, so the risk is operational rather than confirmed malicious.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Credential and Report Handling
medium_findings.description: The workflow asks for tenant and application identifiers, a PFX path, and an environment variable for the PFX password, then generates reports that the skill labels as sensitive. Mishandling the local workspace or logs could expose SharePoint access data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly references certificate authentication inputs, PFX_PASS handling, and sensitive report output. No exfiltration is present, but the confidentiality impact is clear.

medium_findings.title: Module Installation May Use Network Access
medium_findings.description: The skill permits internet access for module installation and provides a PowerShell install command. This can be legitimate, but operators should review module sources and avoid running installs in untrusted environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The install command and rule about module installs indicate allowed network-dependent setup. The file does not show the install script contents, so supply-chain risk remains contextual.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Execution Detections Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings occur in Markdown instruction text and inline command formatting, not Ruby source code. The underlying local command guidance is separately assessed as external command risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is Markdown, and the flagged backticks delimit text or commands. No Ruby file or Ruby execution context is present.

low_findings.title: PowerShell Mentions Without Invocation Are Contextual
low_findings.description: Some PowerShell static matches are descriptive requirements rather than command execution. They explain prerequisites for the audit workflow and do not independently invoke a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These lines describe PowerShell and Python availability. The only concrete shell execution evidence is the command guidance later in the file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detection Is a YAML Syntax False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alert points to the YAML folded description marker. No evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak algorithm was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata syntax for a multi-line description. It does not contain cryptographic code or algorithm configuration.

dangerous_patterns.title: PowerShell and Python Shell Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs the assistant to run a PowerShell module installation script and a Python audit agent command locally. These commands should require operator confirmation and a trusted working tree.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command strings are explicit and match the external command risk factor. The file also restricts activity to local commands, reducing but not removing risk.

content.value_statement: SharePoint permissions are hard to review across sites and batches without repeatable tooling. This skill guides local collection, execution, and report review for SharePoint Online audits.
actual_capabilities: Collects required SharePoint audit inputs before execution.
Checks that PowerShell, Python, and PnP.PowerShell are available locally.
Runs the local module installation and audit agent commands when authorized.
Supports one site URL or a CSV-defined batch audit workflow.
Directs users to generated Markdown or HTML report output.
Reminds users that generated audit reports contain sensitive data.
limitations: Requires local PowerShell 7.4 or newer and Python 3.10 or newer.
Requires certificate authentication details and a PFX password environment variable.
Depends on the external audit agent files being present in the expected repository path.
Does not analyze report contents beyond locating generated output.

use_cases.title: Review One SharePoint Site
use_cases.description: Audit permissions for a single SharePoint Online site and open the generated local report.

use_cases.title: Run Batch Access Reviews
use_cases.description: Use a CSV-defined site list to support repeatable SharePoint permission reviews across multiple locations.

use_cases.title: Prepare Compliance Evidence
use_cases.description: Generate local Markdown or HTML evidence for SharePoint access review activities.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Single Site Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for one SharePoint site.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me audit permissions for one SharePoint Online site. Ask me for the required tenant, application, certificate, domain, and site details first.

prompt_templates.title: Check Local Prerequisites
prompt_templates.scenario: Verify the workstation before running commands.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether my local environment is ready for the SharePoint audit workflow, including PowerShell, Python, PnP.PowerShell, and required inputs.

prompt_templates.title: Run a CSV Batch Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Audit multiple sites from a prepared CSV.
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through a batch SharePoint permissions audit using my CSV file, and confirm each command before execution.

prompt_templates.title: Review Generated Audit Outputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the generated SharePoint audit reports, summarize where they are stored, and remind me how to handle sensitive report files.

output_examples.input: Audit one SharePoint Online site after I provide the required local certificate details.
output_examples.output: A guided checklist of required inputs, prerequisite checks, approved local commands, and the final report location.

output_examples.input: Run a batch audit from my site list.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step batch workflow that confirms the CSV path, runs the local audit agent, and points to generated reports.

output_examples.input: Show me the audit result files after the command succeeds.
output_examples.output: A concise summary of the local report folder and a warning that the files contain sensitive SharePoint data.

best_practices: Run the workflow only from a trusted local repository checkout.
Store the PFX password in the environment and never paste it into chat.
Restrict Sites.Selected permission to Read unless Write is explicitly required.
anti_patterns: Do not run the install or audit commands before reviewing the local scripts.
Do not share generated reports in public channels or unsecured tickets.
Do not grant broader SharePoint permissions than the audit requires.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands on my machine?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides local PowerShell and Python command execution, so review commands before approving them.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: It may use internet access for module installation. The skill otherwise instructs local command use.

faq.question: What credentials are needed?
faq.answer: It asks for tenant and app identifiers, a PFX path, internal domains, and confirmation that PFX_PASS is set.

faq.question: Can it audit multiple SharePoint sites?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports a single site URL or a CSV-defined batch workflow.

faq.question: Are the reports sensitive?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explicitly reminds users that generated SharePoint audit reports contain sensitive data.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a warning because command execution and sensitive local data handling are part of the workflow.
